Hey, what's going on everyone? So phase three hybrid flip sales overview. All right. So warning, first off, it should only be done after you have done phase one and two successfully, meaning you already have recurring revenue that has been stacked up, you already successfully sell into cold traffic, and you decided to add on one on remote to your existing clientele base and you've stacked money. Now, after that point, you've stacked up enough recurring that you can pay your rent and your coaches and yourself with zero front end revenue. At that point, and front end should just be bonus here. So like don't count your front end revenue towards the stability of the business. All right, so this should all of this should be based on the recurring that you've built up. All right. Now, there are two scenarios that you can flip and the reasons that you would do that. Each scenarios is somewhat different. Okay, so if you want to do any if you flip and basically get rid of all of your training at your facility, you would flip your large group and or semi-private into remote all right now when this happens, you are going to lose customers because expectations are being changed, right. And so there's tons of hooks and on offers and ways that we can navigate that stuff. But I just want you to understand that you're going to lose 30%, 40%, sometimes 50% of the clients that you have at your facility when you make this flip. Okay, so you want to make that once you're already at a point of stability. And this would be for somebody who's like, you know, I'm done with the gym, I don't I don't need, you know, like I don't, I don't get a lot from the community. I am kind of ready for this, you know, a next phase of life where I don't have to be at the facility really at all, I can travel more, can be with my, you know, family more. And, you know, that's, and I just want to have the 14 day check ins that my coaches do for me, right, or large group. If they transition to ACP, do very, very well. Okay, they, they just they do better, they do great. It's more personalized attention and they're accustomed to coming to the facility. People who do bootcamp and or large group, everyone have to find it us, and go to remote, tend to struggle more with the transition. Okay, and so I'm just I'm giving you this heads up of the two scenarios. And that's where you have to make the decision as a business owner of what your goals are. If you see your online, your online slash hybrid revenue, and it is just far greater and the amount of effort you put into it as far less, and it's just more exciting for you, then at that point, it's kind of like, cool, well, then let's maximize what we can get out of the gym and convert as many of those people. All right, on the flip side, if you're like, you know, I still like my gym, and I'm cool having it. And I just want to keep getting these people great results. But I do want to get rid of the overhead that's associated with a large group, then pushes many as you can into into HDP, and if it's a price issue, then flip them into remote. Okay. And so that's kind of the two scenarios, and it depends on what your goals are. All right, so go into each of the trains that are subsequent to this, with that understanding of which one you want to do. All right, and you can listen to the recordings for that intent and purpose.
